title: Cipher
description: Decrypt intercepted ciphertexts using classic and modern cryptographic attacks. Covers Caesar, XOR, and weak RSA.
longDescription:
Three encrypted messages are waiting. Each uses a progressively harder cipher: Caesar rotation, single-byte XOR, and textbook RSA with dangerously small primes.

No crypto library is required — brute force, frequency analysis, and basic math will get you through. The flag is assembled from fragments inside each decrypted message.

### What you will practice
Frequency analysis, XOR bruteforcing, RSA factor recovery with small primes, and Python scripting for crypto attacks.
creator: Dock & Root Labs
objectives: Decrypt a Caesar-shifted ciphertext via frequency analysis
Recover the XOR key and decrypt the second message
Factor the RSA modulus and decrypt the third message
